verdict: 2026-07-12 09:17 PDT — The deletion-first shape is modern and appropriately simple for the disproven panel diagnostic: it removes dead machinery, tests, and documentation without introducing a replacement abstraction or dependency. However, the sketch should not retire the overlay breadcrumb until its materially different, app-initiated teardown path is tested or Thomas explicitly accepts losing that potentially valid signal.

findings.title: Overlay retirement extrapolates beyond the evidence
findings.locus: Open question 1 / Design sketch — OverlayApp.jsx and overlay live docs
findings.claim: The observed failure covers Zoom hard-killing or hiding the panel webview, while overlay teardown follows an app-initiated closeRenderingContext call. The story explicitly says the Hide path was not exercised, yet its recommended design deletes overlay-teardown and rewrites documentation that currently treats it as an expected signal. Those lifecycle paths are different enough that the panel result does not establish the overlay instrument is dead.
findings.alternative: Retire only panel-teardown now and retain the shared helper plus overlay caller until a focused Hide-path run establishes whether pagehide is delivered. Alternatively, make successful Hide-path validation an explicit prerequisite to choosing the full-deletion branch.
findings.win: Avoids deleting potentially working overlay teardown telemetry and prevents the live test guide from asserting an unverified behavior change; the decision remains a small, local deletion once evidence exists.
